task: I need to text a coworker about covering the last hour of my shift because something came up. What’s a simple way to word it so the handoff is clear and nothing gets missed?

turns.agent_response:
You can keep it simple and direct:

“Hey, something came up and I need to step out for the last hour of my shift. Can you cover my section? I’ll make sure everything is handed off clearly before I go.”

If you want, I can also give you:
- a more casual version
- a more professional version
- a version that includes exactly what they need to watch for

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I’d want the actual handoff part tighter. Like:

“Hey, something came up and I need you to cover the last hour of my shift. I’ll leave everything organized and tell you what’s still open before I go.”

If you want it to sound less stiff, I’d make it even shorter. And if there’s anything specific — tables, tabs, a running order, whatever — put that in the text so nobody can act confused later.

turns.agent_response:
Got it — tighter and more usable.

**Simple text:**
“Hey, something came up and I need you to cover the last hour of my shift. I’ll leave everything organized and tell you what’s still open before I go.”

**Even shorter:**
“Hey, can you cover my last hour? I’ve got something come up. I’ll leave everything organized and hand off anything still open before I go.”

**If you want to lock it down more:**
“Hey, something came up and I need you to cover the last hour of my shift. I’ll hand off my tables, open tabs, and anything still pending before I leave.”

If you want, I can make it **more casual**, **more professional**, or **restaurant-specific**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s better. The last one is the one I’d use.

If I was sending it, I’d probably do:

“Hey, something came up and I need you to cover my last hour. I’ll hand off my tables, open tabs, and anything still pending before I leave.”

Clean enough. No extra chatter.
